rationale_for_baseline_tier: Once DVT, compartment syndrome and arterial ischaemia are excluded on history, an exercise-associated cramp is benign and self-managed with stretch, rehydration and analgesia (T0). Hard safety-nets to T4/T5 cover the excluded vascular/compartment emergencies and rhabdomyolysis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Whole-leg (diffuse) swelling, warmth, redness or calf tenderness persisting at rest, especially with breathlessness or chest pain
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your whole calf becomes swollen, warm, red or tender even when relaxed — or you get breathless or chest pain — go to the Emergency Department (call 000 if breathless).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe worsening tense calf swelling with numbness/tingling, or a cold, pale foot
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the calf becomes tensely swollen with numbness or tingling, or your foot turns cold and pale — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Dark tea/cola-coloured urine or markedly reduced urine output
escalation_edges.rationale: Rhabdomyolysis with risk of acute kidney injury.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your urine turns dark like tea or cola — go to the Emergency Department the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure they can rest and rehydrate, and have a plan to reach ED if red flags develop.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Stretch the calf and hold, drink an electrolyte solution, rest the leg and take paracetamol for soreness. It should settle, with a bruised feeling for a couple of days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; ED for the specific leg/urine red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 for a cold pale foot or tense swollen calf with numbness; ED for diffuse leg swelling or dark urine.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Exercise-associated gastrocnemius cramp; DVT/compartment/ischaemia and rhabdomyolysis screened negative; conservative management.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing to cramp with no DVT/compartment/ischaemia screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unrecognised DVT embolises to the lungs, or a compartment syndrome progresses to limb loss.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No rhabdomyolysis (dark-urine) safety-net after severe exertion.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a benign cramp with a fully negative vascular screen — unnecessary.
